description: Each bar's width is proportional to its sales value, meaning the data is encoded twice — once through height and once through width. As a result, the perceived area of each bar grows quadratically with the actual value, making the differences between top-selling and lower-selling sub-categories appear far more dramatic than they truly are. A reader comparing "Chairs" to "Copiers" will perceive an area ratio much larger than the roughly 2:1 ratio that exists in the actual sales figures.
